description: To breathe: the act 'whunei' (breath) names the substance of. The breath-family gains its verb — 'whesu' (blow) directs air outward at the world; 'whunema' is the body's own tide, in and out.
sound_symbolism: Built on 'whunei': the voiceless 'whu' is air itself, and 'ne'–'ma' is the grounding rhythm of in and out — the two nasals rocking like the breath they carry.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'no moli whunema.' (Breathe gently.) 'mia thesua whunema.' (I breathe mindfully.) With aspect: 'shia si whunema.' (They are breathing.) The meditation instruction in two words: 'no whunema.'

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Every tradition of attention begins here: the verb of the first instruction. That Phi lacked it until now is the gap inventory's best argument for itself.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Work songs, birthing rooms, and cold mornings all ran on instructed breathing long before it was a practice — breathe was always a verb people said to each other.

tags.activity: The act beneath every other act
tags.wisdom: The verb of the first instruction
